published.title: How to Set Up a Fully Local Voice Assistant in Home Assistant (No Cloud, No Alexa)
targetKeywords: home assistant local voice assistant no cloud
home assistant voice PE privacy local
published.metaDescription: Set up a fully local, cloud-free voice assistant in Home Assistant using Voice PE, Wyoming protocol, Piper TTS, and openWakeWord—step-by-step.
published.angle: The Home Assistant Voice Preview Edition hardware just shipped and r/homeassistant is buzzing—show readers exactly how to wire up the full local stack (Voice PE → Wyoming → Whisper STT → Piper TTS → openWakeWord) so every spoken command stays on their LAN, with zero Alexa, zero Google, zero Nabu Casa cloud required.

published.title: How to Add Battery-Powered mmWave Presence Sensors to Home Assistant via Matter & Thread (No Cloud)
targetKeywords: Matter over Thread mmWave presence sensor
published.metaDescription: Set up Aqara FP300 or Meross MS605 mmWave sensors over Thread in Home Assistant—fully local, zero cloud, step-by-step.
published.angle: The first wave of battery-powered Matter-over-Thread mmWave sensors (Aqara FP300, Meross MS605) just launched—show readers exactly how to pair them locally in Home Assistant using an OpenThread border router, with no cloud account required and no proprietary hub.

keywordRank.Matter over Thread mmWave presence senso: not ranked — tarahome.ai not found in results (top results: SmartHomeScene, MatterAlpha, Amazon)
keywordRank.Aqara FP300 Home Assistant local: not ranked — tarahome.ai not found in results (top results: HA Community, Aqara Forum, derekseaman.com)

lastSeoCheck.recommendation: Post is live and content is solid, but Google has not indexed it yet (normal for a post published 2026-03-08; expect 24–72h). Once indexed, the main competition for 'Matter over Thread mmWave presence sensor' is SmartHomeScene and MatterAlpha — both well-established; tarahome.ai should pursue backlinks from HA Community forum threads and the Aqara forum where FP300 discussions are active. For 'Aqara FP300 Home Assistant local', derekseaman.com dominates with detailed how-tos; differentiation via the Thread/Matter angle (vs. his Zigbee-only focus) is the right strategy and already reflected in the post. Recommend submitting the URL to Google Search Console for faster indexing, and adding internal links from any existing tarahome.ai content.
